name: Watermelon
image_alt: Sliced radish showing green exterior and vivid pink interior
description: A large Chinese daikon-type radish with pale green exterior and a vivid magenta-pink interior — resembling the flesh of a watermelon when sliced. Mild, slightly sweet flavor with a satisfying crunch.
history: A traditional Chinese radish (xinlimei) grown for centuries in China. Brought to American farmers markets in the early 2000s where its dramatic appearance made it an instant sensation among chefs and specialty growers.
seasonal_quirks: A fall radish — plant in late summer for fall harvest. Spring-planted roots bolt before developing properly. Cool weather intensifies the pink interior color. Stores well in cold conditions.
pests_diseases: relatively less pest pressure than spring types
harvest_cues: 2–3 inches in diameter. Slice to check interior color — vivid pink means ready. Green exterior with slight give indicates maturity.
variety_notes: Slice thin for maximum visual impact — the color gradient from green edge to pink center is most striking in thin rounds. Best used raw or very quickly pickled to preserve color.
preservation: Quick pickle in rice vinegar — color holds beautifully. Stores whole in cold, moist conditions for several months.
